objective: Enforce the planner write-boundary so Tier 1 planning roles may create planning, review, and handoff artifacts only, while non-planning diffs require accepted pinned-agent handoff evidence and active exceptions for same-model or same-family implementers.
scope_boundary: Implement a machine-checkable planning artifact allowlist using execution-scope allowed write paths.
Extend execution-scope validation with diff-file input for CI and optional pinned-agent handoff evidence.
Require accepted handoff evidence for non-planning diffs and active exception references for same-model or same-family implementers.
Wire strict enforcement into governance CI while keeping local hook behavior early-warning only for the planner-boundary checker.
Document delegated mode, local mirror mode, handoff evidence, and exception behavior.
Add regression tests for planning-only pass, non-planning fail without handoff, accepted handoff pass, planner-model implementer fail without exception, and exception pass.
out_of_scope: Reopening the #241 graphify artifact contract implementation.
Adding required fields to historical structured agent cycle plans.
Cryptographically proving who typed a diff.
Replacing GitHub Issues as the canonical execution backlog.
Changing unrelated Society of Minds packet validation invariants.
Popping or applying the mixed shared-checkout stash into this branch.
research_summary: Issue #242 is the narrow follow-up from #241. Research found that #226 already added a governance-surface branch gate requiring issue-specific structured plans, implementation-ready handoff, and accepted alternate review for governance-surface paths. The remaining gap is file-scope planner/non-planning enforcement: prose rules say planners should not implement, but no strict CI gate yet requires accepted pinned-agent handoff evidence for non-planning diffs. The lowest-risk implementation is to extend `scripts/overlord/assert_execution_scope.py`, because it already owns expected root, expected branch, forbidden roots, and allowed write paths. Historical structured-plan compatibility is preserved by leaving `docs/schemas/structured-agent-cycle-plan.schema.json` unchanged and putting optional `execution_mode` plus `handoff_evidence` in execution-scope JSON.

sprints.name: Execution-Scope Contract
sprints.goal: Make execution-scope artifacts encode the planner write-boundary and accepted pinned-agent handoff evidence without breaking historical structured plans.
tasks: Add optional execution-mode parsing to execution-scope payloads.
Add optional handoff evidence with planner model, implementer model, status, accepted timestamp, evidence paths, active exception reference, and exception expiry.
Treat missing execution mode as the current dirty-tree scope mode for backward compatibility.
Require accepted handoff evidence for non-planning execution modes.
Require active exception evidence when planner and implementer model or family are the same.
acceptance_criteria: Existing execution-scope JSON artifacts still validate unchanged.
Planning-only scopes only permit paths in `allowed_write_paths`.
Non-planning scopes fail without accepted handoff evidence.
Same-model or same-family planner/implementer evidence fails without active exception.

sprints.name: CI and Hook Wiring
sprints.goal: Make CI authoritative for planner-boundary enforcement while local hooks surface early warnings.
tasks: Add `--changed-files-file` support to `assert_execution_scope.py` for PR diff enforcement.
Wire governance CI to call the checker after changed-file resolution.
Keep hook warnings non-authoritative for this boundary while preserving existing new-code routing blocks.
Ensure diff-mode and dirty-tree mode share path normalization and allowed-path semantics.
acceptance_criteria: PR CI blocks non-planning diffs that lack accepted pinned-agent handoff evidence.
Local hook output identifies planner-boundary drift without being the authoritative merge gate.
Renames and copied paths are normalized consistently with dirty-tree mode.

sprints.name: Documentation and Audit Evidence
sprints.goal: Record the rule, the process gap, and the operator-facing handoff path.
tasks: Document delegated mode versus local mirror mode.
Document planning allowlist, handoff evidence fields, exception reference, and expiry behavior.
Register the gate in feature, service, and data dictionary docs.
Update issue #242 with research findings, accepted plan, and validation evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Docs state planners may create planning/review/handoff artifacts only.
Docs state non-planning diffs require accepted pinned-agent handoff evidence.
Audit findings identify why the earlier issue was not gated.
Issue #242 contains the implementation plan and evidence links.

specialist_reviews.focus: Existing validator, PR gate, hook, SoM packet, and execution-scope enforcement map.
specialist_reviews.summary: The active #226 enforcement spine exists, but #242 should put true file-scope planner-boundary enforcement in `assert_execution_scope.py` and wire it into CI.

specialist_reviews.focus: Patch shape, schema compatibility, CI/hook wiring, and required test cases.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended extending execution-scope artifacts and checker behavior instead of structured-plan schema, keeping CI strict and hooks warning-only for the new boundary.

specialist_reviews.focus: Why the planner/non-planning issue was not previously gated and where repo rules deviated from enforcement.
specialist_reviews.summary: Found the root gap was prose-only planner boundary plus narrow local hook behavior that blocked new code files but allowed existing-file and docs/config/data edits.

alternate_model_review.summary: Prior alternate-family review approved keeping #242 separate from #241 with required changes: planning allowlist, exception mechanism, warning-to-strict transition, handoff evidence schema, and strict hook/PR enforcement. This plan incorporates those requirements while keeping local hooks warning-only for the new boundary and CI authoritative.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement #242 only: execution-scope planner-boundary enforcement, CI strict gate, hook warning path, focused tests, docs, and tracker updates. Do not include #241 graphify changes or shared-checkout stash content.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Hand implementation to pinned worker agents with write ownership for assert_execution_scope/tests, CI/hook wiring, and docs. Run validation gates before review and PR.

material_deviation_rules: If structured-plan schema changes appear necessary, stop and produce an alternate-family review update before implementation because historical plans must remain valid.
If active exception behavior cannot be made concrete with reference and expiry, fail closed rather than accepting freeform exception text.
If hook and CI behavior diverge, CI remains authoritative and the hook must be documented as warning-only for this boundary.
If #241 graphify contract files become part of the diff, remove them unless the change is a tracker-only closeout repair explicitly approved for this branch.
If the shared checkout stash is needed, extract only reviewed files into a separate issue-backed branch; do not pop it into #242.
approved_by: spawned research agents: Banach, Dewey, James
